content_en:
(4) Many of the children then begin to disobey their fathers and mothers and refuse to abide by the good customs and morals that have been passed down.

(5) The guardian betrays his ward by refusing to marry her to one who is pious and qualified but whom he believes will not pay a large dower. He refuses that person out of hopes that he will find someone who will pay a larger dower even though his religion and character is not pleasing. He is not working for the happiness of the woman he is in charge of. In addition to this being a type of deception or betrayal, he is leaving that woman in suspense. If he does that often, he must be considered an evil doer, lacking in faith, who is not considered trustworthy until he repents.

(6) Burdening the husband beyond what he can bear [by such exorbitant dowers] produces a hatred in his heart for his wife since his material means are now very limited due to her. The goal of marriage is happiness and not this unhappiness [that is produced by such dowers].!

Now that the causes of exorbitant dowers and their painful consequences have been made clear, we must clarify the ruling of such dowers and what the scholars have said about them.

' Al-Zawaaj wa al-Muboor, pp. 57-58; Min Qahaaya al-Zawaaj, pp. 71-72.

‘The Figh of Marriage

The Ruling Concerning Exorbitant Dowers

Among the scholars, there are three opinions on this issue: (1) Exorbitant dowers are permissible.

(2) Exorbitant dowers are not permissible.

(3) Different cases need to be discussed separately.

The First Opinion: The first opinion states that exorbitant dowers are permissible. An evidence for this view is the following verse of the Quran,

“Tf you have given one of them a great amount [gintaar], do not take any of it back” (al-Nisaa 20). While commenting on this verse, ibn Katheer stated, “In this verse there is an indication that it is allowed to give a great deal of wealth as a dower.”! Al-Qurtubi said in his commentary, “This verse is evidence that it is allowed to be exorbitant in dowers as Allah does not give as an example except what is permissible.”?

In a_ public address, Umar stated, “Listen! Do not become exorbitant when it comes to the dowers of women. Verily, if such a thing were noble in this world or an act of obedience to Allah, the first one to demand such would be the Prophet (peace be upon him). However, the Prophet (peace be upon him) never gave in dower to his wives or asked for dower for his daughters anything in excess of twelve augiyah.” A woman stood up to him and said, “O Umar, Allah has given us and you forbid us. Didn’t Allah say in the Quran, ‘ If you have given one of them a great amount [gintaar], do not take any of it back’ [al-Nisaa 20}?” Then Umar said, “A woman is correct and Umar is mistaken.” Then he stopped his prohibiting them.

“The dower, according to the shareeah, is a gift and offering. Thus, it has no prescribed limit. People differ with respect to being rich

' Tafseer al-Quran al-Adbeem by Abu al-Fidaa ibn Katheer, vol. 1, p. 466 (Daar Ihyaa al-Kutub al-I!miyah, third edition, 1375 A.H.). -Jaami li-Abkaam al-Quran by al-Qurtubi, vol. 5, pp. 99-100.

‘The Figh of Marriage

or poor. So the shareeah has left everyone to set its limit according to their ability.”

The Second Opinion: The second opinion is that it is not allowed to have exorbitant dowers. The followers of this view reply to the verse that was used as evidence by the people of the first opinion by saying that the verse is irrelevant to this issue for the following reasons:

First, the example of a qintaar [a large amount of wealth] is only an exaggerated method to stress the point that even if one had given them a great deal of wealth, he cannot take back any portion of it. It is similar in vein to the Prophet’s (peace be upon him) statement,

“For whoever builds a mosque for the sake of Allah the size of a grouse’s nest, Allah will build for him a house in Paradise.”? “But there cannot be a mosque the size of a grouse’s nest.”

Second, it is not necessary that when one thing is stated as a conditional aspect for something else that the first thing is in itself permissible. Allah’s saying, “You had given them,” does not indicate that it is permissible to give such an amount. It is like the Prophet’s statement,

Oi oy Sh ee Tiss “If someone has a relative killed, he has two choices: either he can be paid the blood money or he may exact retribution.”* Furthermore, the most that one can say about this verse is that it allows the one who has the ability to pay a large dower to pay sucha dower. However, it does not mean that the one who does not have such

1 Tafseer Ayaat al-Abkaam by al-Saabooni, vol. 1, p. 453, first edition, 1391 .H./1972, Damascus. Recorded by ibn Maajah (#738) and ibn Khuzaimah (#1292) on the authority of bir ibn Abdullah. Al-Boosairi said, "Its chain is sabib." 74h -Jaami li-Abkaam al-Quran by al Qurtubi, vol. 5, -101. * Recorded by al-Bukhari (#6880), Muslim Cate ‘Abu Dawood (#4505), alNasaai (vol. 8, p. 38) and al-Baihagi in al-Sunan alKubra (vol. 8, p. 52).

The Figh of Marriage

ability may be forced to pay that amount. This is indicated by the Prophet (peace be upon him) objecting to Abu Hadrad al-Aslami when he came to the Prophet (peace be upon him) to seek assistance in paying the dower of his wife. The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) asked him, “What is the amount of her dower?” He said, “Two hundred dirhams.” The Prophet (peace be upon him) then said, “If you were to dig out [the silver] in the valley of Bathaan, you would not exceed that amount.” Recorded by al-Haakim who said that its chain is sahih although al-Bukhari and Muslim did not record it. Al-Dhahabi concurred with his assessment.'

He also objected to the woman from the Ansar who got married with the dower being four awaag [of silver] as that was not appropriate for her situation. Muslim recorded in his Sahih? from Abu Hurairah who said: A man came to the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) and said, “I am getting married to a woman from the Ansar.” The Prophet (peace be upon him) said to him, “Have you taken a look at her for in the eyes of the Ansaar there is something (unpleasant for those not used to it]?” He said, “I have taken a look at her.” He then said, “What was the amount [of the dower] for the marriage?” He replied, “Four ugiyas.” The Prophet (peace be upon him) then said to him, “With four ugiyas? It is as if you dig out silver from the side of this mountain [and that is how you plan on paying this great amount]. We do not have anything we can give you. However, we may send you ona military expedition and you may end up getting something [that is, some booty].” The Prophet (peace be upon him) sent an expedition to Banu Abs and he sent that man along with them.

Al-Nawawi wrote in his commentary to Sahih Muslim, “The meaning of those words is the disapproval of increasing the dower telative to the situation of the husband.”

As for the story wherein Umar prohibits exorbitant dowers and then the woman refutes what he stated, although it is very famous and spread among the people, all of its chains are questionable and many

3 Al-Mustadrak ala al-Saheebain by al-Haakim al-Naisaboori, vol. 2, p. 178.

No. 1424, The Book of Marriage, Chapter on looking at the face and hands of the oman one desires to wed.

Sharh Sabeeh Muslim by al-Nawawi, vol. 9, p. 210.

‘The Figh of Marriage

scholars have doubted it. It should be noted that it is not recorded in any source work that dates from close to the time of Umar. Furthermore, it is not recorded in the many relied upon source works of hadith. The four companions of the Sunan works [Abu Dawood, al-Nasaai, al-Tirmidhi and ibn Maajah] all avoided recording it. Many of the leading scholars of hadith, those who recorded Umar’s prohibition of exorbitant dowers, only record the portion where Umar speaks about the dowers related to the Prophet (peace be upon him) [and they do not mention anything of the rest of the story concerning that woman].

In Ahkaam al-Quran,! Abu Bakr ibn al-Arabi clearly stated that the well-established and known narration from Umar is that in which no woman objects to what he stated.

Furthermore, the hadith, as recorded by the compilers of the Sunan who did not mention the story of the woman, has been narrated through al-Ajfaa ibn Naseeb and _ his hadith are not founded and some say that there is some doubt about him.?

Some record a narration that contradicts the narration that states that a woman refuted Umar with the verse she quoted. Some, instead of having a woman rebut Umar, have the additional words, that were mentioned earlier, “A man goes to such an extreme in getting a dower for his wife that there develops an enmity for her in him, and he says, ‘I burdened myself [to the point of even providing] the rope of the waterskin for you.’””3

2 Tabdbeeb al-Tabdbeeb by ibn Hajr al-Asqilaani, vol. 14, p. 165, #790. [It is not clear to. this translator the point the author is trying to make here. Earlier, he stated in a footnote that the narration from Umar is confirmed and authentic. The questionable aspect is the additional portion, narrated by some, in which a woman confronted Umar concerning what he had said. The paragraph above does nothing to support the contention that the additional portion is not authentic, as the author has stated and shall continue to demonstrate. ‘Allah knows best —JZ]

Recorded by Abu Dawood (#2106) in the Book of Marriage, Chapter on the dower; al-Tirmidhi (#1114) in the Book of Marriage, Chapter on what has been related concerning the dower of women; al-Nasaai (vol. 6, p. 117) in the Book of Marriage, Chapter on being just in the dowers, and the wording above is from him; ibn Maajah ie 887); Ahmad in al-Musnad (#285, 278, 340); and al-Haakim in al-Mustadrak vol. 2, p. 175). It is a narration that is confirmed and authentic.

‘The Fig of Marriage

Al-Albaani discusses in Irwaa al-Ghaleel' the authenticity of the story of Umar prohibiting exorbitant dowers. He stated,
content_id:
(4) Banyak anak kemudian mulai durhaka kepada ayah dan ibu mereka serta menolak untuk mematuhi adat istiadat dan akhlak yang baik yang telah diwariskan.

(5) Wali mengkhianati anak perempuan yang berada di bawah asuhannya dengan menolak menikahkannya dengan orang yang saleh dan layak, namun yang menurutnya tidak akan membayar mahar yang besar. Ia menolak orang tersebut dengan harapan menemukan seseorang yang akan membayar mahar lebih besar, meskipun agama dan akhlaknya tidak memuaskan. Ia tidak bekerja demi kebahagiaan wanita yang berada dalam tanggung jawabnya. Selain menjadi bentuk penipuan atau pengkhianatan, ia pun membiarkan wanita itu dalam keadaan terkatung-katung. Jika ia melakukan hal itu berulang kali, ia harus dianggap sebagai pelaku kemungkaran, yang lemah iman, dan tidak dapat dipercaya hingga ia bertaubat.

(6) Membebankan suami melebihi kemampuannya [dengan mahar yang berlebihan] menimbulkan kebencian dalam hatinya terhadap istrinya, karena kemampuan materialnya kini sangat terbatas akibatnya. Tujuan pernikahan adalah kebahagiaan, bukan ketidakbahagiaan seperti ini [yang ditimbulkan oleh mahar semacam itu].

Setelah sebab-sebab mahar yang berlebihan dan akibat-akibat menyakitkan yang ditimbulkannya telah dijelaskan, kita harus memperjelas hukum mahar semacam itu dan apa yang dikatakan para ulama tentangnya.

' Al-Zawaaj wa al-Muboor, hlm. 57-58; Min Qahaaya al-Zawaaj, hlm. 71-72.

'Fiqh Pernikahan

Hukum Mahar yang Berlebihan

Di antara para ulama, terdapat tiga pendapat mengenai masalah ini: (1) Mahar yang berlebihan diperbolehkan.

(2) Mahar yang berlebihan tidak diperbolehkan.

(3) Kasus-kasus yang berbeda perlu dibahas secara terpisah.

Pendapat Pertama: Pendapat pertama menyatakan bahwa mahar yang berlebihan diperbolehkan. Salah satu dalil untuk pandangan ini adalah ayat Quran berikut:

"Jika kamu telah memberikan kepada salah seorang di antara mereka harta yang banyak [qintaar], maka janganlah kamu mengambil kembali sedikit pun darinya" (al-Nisaa: 20). Dalam menafsirkan ayat ini, Ibnu Katsir menyatakan, "Dalam ayat ini terdapat petunjuk bahwa diperbolehkan memberikan harta yang banyak sebagai mahar."! Al-Qurtubi berkata dalam tafsirnya, "Ayat ini merupakan dalil bahwa berlebih-lebihan dalam mahar diperbolehkan, karena Allah tidak memberikan contoh kecuali sesuatu yang diperbolehkan."²

Dalam sebuah pidato umum, Umar menyatakan, "Dengarkan! Janganlah kalian berlebih-lebihan dalam mahar wanita. Sesungguhnya, jika hal itu mulia di dunia ini atau merupakan ketaatan kepada Allah, maka orang pertama yang akan menuntutnya adalah Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam). Namun, Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) tidak pernah memberikan mahar kepada istri-istrinya atau meminta mahar untuk putri-putrinya melebihi dua belas uqiyah." Seorang wanita berdiri dan berkata, "Wahai Umar, Allah telah memberikan kepada kami namun engkau melarang kami. Bukankah Allah berfirman dalam Quran, 'Jika kamu telah memberikan kepada salah seorang di antara mereka harta yang banyak [qintaar], maka janganlah kamu mengambil kembali sedikit pun darinya' [al-Nisaa: 20]?" Maka Umar berkata, "Wanita itu benar dan Umar keliru." Kemudian ia menghentikan larangannya tersebut.

"Mahar, menurut syariah, adalah hadiah dan pemberian. Dengan demikian, tidak ada batasan yang ditentukan untuknya. Manusia berbeda-beda dalam hal kekayaan

' Tafseer al-Quran al-Adheem oleh Abu al-Fidaa ibn Katheer, vol. 1, hlm. 466 (Daar Ihyaa al-Kutub al-Ilmiyah, edisi ketiga, 1375 H.). ²Al-Jaami li-Ahkaam al-Quran oleh al-Qurtubi, vol. 5, hlm. 99-100.

'Fiqh Pernikahan

dan kemiskinan. Maka syariah membiarkan setiap orang menentukan batasannya sesuai dengan kemampuannya."

Pendapat Kedua: Pendapat kedua adalah bahwa mahar yang berlebihan tidak diperbolehkan. Para pengikut pandangan ini menjawab ayat yang dijadikan dalil oleh penganut pendapat pertama dengan menyatakan bahwa ayat tersebut tidak relevan dengan masalah ini karena alasan-alasan berikut:

Pertama, contoh qintaar [harta dalam jumlah besar] hanyalah cara berlebih-lebihan untuk menekankan bahwa meskipun seseorang telah memberikan harta yang banyak, ia tidak boleh mengambil kembali sedikit pun darinya. Hal ini serupa dengan sabda Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam),

"Barangsiapa membangun masjid karena Allah seukuran sarang burung qathaa, Allah akan membangunkan baginya sebuah rumah di surga."² "Namun tidak mungkin ada masjid seukuran sarang burung qathaa."

Kedua, tidak harus bahwa ketika suatu hal disebutkan sebagai aspek syarat bagi hal lain, berarti hal pertama itu sendiri diperbolehkan. Firman Allah, "Kamu telah memberikan kepada mereka," tidak menunjukkan bahwa diperbolehkan memberikan jumlah sebesar itu. Ini seperti sabda Nabi,

أَيُّ شَيْءٍ... "Jika seseorang memiliki kerabat yang terbunuh, ia memiliki dua pilihan: ia dapat menerima diyat atau menuntut qishash."⁴ Selanjutnya, paling jauh yang dapat dikatakan tentang ayat ini adalah bahwa ia memperbolehkan orang yang mampu membayar mahar yang besar untuk membayar mahar sebesar itu. Namun, bukan berarti orang yang tidak memiliki kemampuan tersebut

¹ Tafseer Ayaat al-Ahkaam oleh al-Saabooni, vol. 1, hlm. 453, edisi pertama, 1391 H./1972, Damaskus. Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Maajah (#738) dan Ibnu Khuzaimah (#1292) dari Jabir ibn Abdullah. Al-Boosairi berkata, "Sanadnya shahih." ²Al-Jaami li-Ahkaam al-Quran oleh al-Qurtubi, vol. 5, hlm. 101. ⁴ Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari (#6880), Muslim, Abu Dawood (#4505), al-Nasaai (vol. 8, hlm. 38) dan al-Baihaqi dalam al-Sunan al-Kubra (vol. 8, hlm. 52).

Fiqh Pernikahan

dapat dipaksa membayar jumlah tersebut. Hal ini ditunjukkan oleh keberatan Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) kepada Abu Hadrad al-Aslami ketika ia datang kepada Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) untuk meminta bantuan dalam membayar mahar istrinya. Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bertanya kepadanya, "Berapa jumlah maharnya?" Ia menjawab, "Dua ratus dirham." Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) kemudian bersabda, "Seandainya kamu menggali [perak] di lembah Bathaan, kamu tidak akan mendapatkan sebanyak itu." Diriwayatkan oleh al-Haakim yang menyatakan bahwa sanadnya shahih meskipun al-Bukhari dan Muslim tidak memuatnya. Al-Dzahabi sependapat dengan penilaiannya tersebut.¹

Beliau juga berkeberatan terhadap wanita dari kaum Ansar yang menikah dengan mahar empat uqiyah [perak] karena hal itu tidak sesuai dengan kondisinya. Muslim meriwayatkan dalam Shahihnya² dari Abu Hurairah yang berkata: Seorang laki-laki datang kepada Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) dan berkata, "Saya akan menikahi seorang wanita dari kaum Ansar." Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda kepadanya, "Apakah kamu telah melihatnya, karena pada mata kaum Ansar ada sesuatu [yang tidak menyenangkan bagi yang tidak terbiasa]?" Ia menjawab, "Saya telah melihatnya." Beliau kemudian bersabda, "Berapa jumlah [mahar] untuk pernikahan itu?" Ia menjawab, "Empat uqiyah." Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) kemudian bersabda kepadanya, "Dengan empat uqiyah? Seolah-olah kamu menggali perak dari sisi gunung ini [dan begitulah cara kamu berencana membayar jumlah yang besar ini]. Kami tidak memiliki sesuatu yang dapat kami berikan kepadamu. Namun, kami mungkin akan mengutusmu dalam ekspedisi militer dan kamu mungkin mendapatkan sesuatu [yaitu ghanimah]." Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) kemudian mengutus ekspedisi ke Bani Abs dan ia mengutus laki-laki itu bersama mereka.

Al-Nawawi menulis dalam syarahnya terhadap Shahih Muslim, "Makna kata-kata tersebut adalah ketidaksetujuan terhadap peningkatan mahar yang tidak sesuai dengan kemampuan suami."

Adapun kisah di mana Umar melarang mahar yang berlebihan kemudian seorang wanita membantah apa yang ia katakan, meskipun kisah ini sangat terkenal dan tersebar di kalangan masyarakat, seluruh sanadnya dipertanyakan dan banyak

³ Al-Mustadrak ala al-Shahihain oleh al-Haakim al-Naisaboori, vol. 2, hlm. 178.

No. 1424, Kitab Nikah, Bab tentang melihat wajah dan tangan wanita yang ingin dinikahi.

Syarh Shahih Muslim oleh al-Nawawi, vol. 9, hlm. 210.

'Fiqh Pernikahan

ulama yang meragukannya. Perlu dicatat bahwa kisah ini tidak tercatat dalam sumber mana pun yang berasal dari masa dekat dengan zaman Umar. Selanjutnya, kisah ini tidak tercatat dalam banyak kitab hadith yang menjadi rujukan utama. Empat sahabat penyusun kitab Sunan [Abu Dawood, al-Nasaai, al-Tirmidzi dan Ibnu Maajah] semuanya menghindari pencatatannya. Banyak ulama hadith terkemuka, mereka yang mencatat larangan Umar terhadap mahar yang berlebihan, hanya mencatat bagian di mana Umar berbicara tentang mahar yang berkaitan dengan Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) [dan mereka tidak menyebutkan apa pun dari sisa kisah mengenai wanita tersebut].

Dalam Ahkaam al-Quran,¹ Abu Bakr ibn al-Arabi dengan tegas menyatakan bahwa riwayat yang mapan dan dikenal dari Umar adalah riwayat yang di dalamnya tidak ada wanita yang membantah apa yang ia katakan.

Selanjutnya, hadith, sebagaimana diriwayatkan oleh para penyusun kitab Sunan yang tidak menyebutkan kisah wanita tersebut, telah diriwayatkan melalui al-Ajfaa ibn Naseeb dan hadith-hadithnya tidak memiliki landasan yang kuat, dan sebagian mengatakan bahwa ada keraguan tentang dirinya.²

Sebagian riwayat mencatat narasi yang bertentangan dengan narasi yang menyatakan bahwa seorang wanita membantah Umar dengan ayat yang ia kutip. Sebagian, alih-alih menampilkan seorang wanita yang membantah Umar, memuat tambahan kata-kata yang telah disebutkan sebelumnya, "Seorang laki-laki pergi ke tingkat yang berlebihan dalam menetapkan mahar bagi istrinya sehingga timbul rasa permusuhan terhadapnya dalam dirinya, dan ia berkata, 'Aku membebankan diriku [sampai-sampai menyediakan] tali timba air untukmu.'"

2 Tahdzib al-Tahdzib karya Ibn Hajar al-Asqalaani, jilid 14, hlm. 165, #790. [Tidak jelas bagi penerjemah ini maksud yang ingin disampaikan penulis di sini. Sebelumnya, ia menyatakan dalam catatan kaki bahwa riwayat dari Umar telah terkonfirmasi dan sahih. Aspek yang dipermasalahkan adalah bagian tambahan, yang diriwayatkan oleh sebagian, di mana seorang wanita mengkonfrontasi Umar mengenai apa yang telah ia katakan. Paragraf di atas tidak mendukung pernyataan bahwa bagian tambahan tersebut tidak sahih, sebagaimana telah dinyatakan penulis dan akan terus ia buktikan. 'Wallahu a'lam —JZ]

Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud (#2106) dalam Kitab Nikah, Bab tentang mahar; al-Tirmidzi (#1114) dalam Kitab Nikah, Bab tentang apa yang diriwayatkan mengenai mahar wanita; al-Nasaai (jilid 6, hlm. 117) dalam Kitab Nikah, Bab tentang berlaku adil dalam mahar, dan redaksi di atas berasal darinya; Ibn Majah (#887); Ahmad dalam al-Musnad (#285, 278, 340); dan al-Hakim dalam al-Mustadrak (jilid 2, hlm. 175). Ini adalah narasi yang terkonfirmasi dan sahih.

'Fiqh Pernikahan

Al-Albaani membahas dalam Irwa al-Ghaleel kesahihan kisah Umar yang melarang mahar yang berlebihan. Ia menyatakan,
content_ar:
(4) Many of the children then begin to disobey their fathers and mothers and refuse to abide by the good customs and morals that have been passed down.

(5) The guardian betrays his ward by refusing to marry her to one who is pious and qualified but whom he believes will not pay a large dower. He refuses that person out of hopes that he will find someone who will pay a larger dower even though his religion and character is not pleasing. He is not working for the happiness of the woman he is in charge of. In addition to this being a type of deception or betrayal, he is leaving that woman in suspense. If he does that often, he must be considered an evil doer, lacking in faith, who is not considered trustworthy until he repents.

(6) Burdening the husband beyond what he can bear [by such exorbitant dowers] produces a hatred in his heart for his wife since his material means are now very limited due to her. The goal of marriage is happiness and not this unhappiness [that is produced by such dowers].!

Now that the causes of exorbitant dowers and their painful consequences have been made clear, we must clarify the ruling of such dowers and what the scholars have said about them.

' Al-Zawaaj wa al-Muboor, pp. 57-58; Min Qahaaya al-Zawaaj, pp. 71-72.

‘The Figh of Marriage

The Ruling Concerning Exorbitant Dowers

Among the scholars, there are three opinions on this issue: (1) Exorbitant dowers are permissible.

(2) Exorbitant dowers are not permissible.

(3) Different cases need to be discussed separately.

The First Opinion: The first opinion states that exorbitant dowers are permissible. An evidence for this view is the following verse of the القرآن,

“Tf you have given one of them a great amount [gintaar], do not take any of it back” (al-Nisaa 20). While commenting on this verse, ibn Katheer stated, “In this verse there is an indication that it is allowed to give a great deal of wealth as a dower.”! Al-Qurtubi said in his commentary, “This verse is evidence that it is allowed to be exorbitant in dowers as الله does not give as an example except what is permissible.”?

In a_ public address, Umar stated, “Listen! Do not become exorbitant when it comes to the dowers of women. Verily, if such a thing were noble in this world or an act of obedience to الله, the first one to demand such would be النبي ﷺ (عليه السلام). However, النبي ﷺ (عليه السلام) never gave in dower to his wives or asked for dower for his daughters anything in excess of twelve augiyah.” A woman stood up to him and said, “O Umar, الله has given us and you forbid us. Didn’t الله say in the القرآن, ‘ If you have given one of them a great amount [gintaar], do not take any of it back’ [al-Nisaa 20}?” Then Umar said, “A woman is correct and Umar is mistaken.” Then he stopped his prohibiting them.

“The dower, according to the shareeah, is a gift and offering. Thus, it has no prescribed limit. People differ with respect to being rich

' Tafseer al-القرآن al-Adbeem by Abu al-Fidaa ibn Katheer, vol. 1, p. 466 (Daar Ihyaa al-Kutub al-I!miyah, third edition, 1375 A.H.). -Jaami li-Abkaam al-القرآن by al-Qurtubi, vol. 5, pp. 99-100.

‘The Figh of Marriage

or poor. So the shareeah has left everyone to set its limit according to their ability.”

The Second Opinion: The second opinion is that it is not allowed to have exorbitant dowers. The followers of this view reply to the verse that was used as evidence by the people of the first opinion by saying that the verse is irrelevant to this issue for the following reasons:

First, the example of a qintaar [a large amount of wealth] is only an exaggerated method to stress the point that even if one had given them a great deal of wealth, he cannot take back any portion of it. It is similar in vein to النبي ﷺ’s (عليه السلام) statement,

“For whoever builds a mosque for the sake of الله the size of a grouse’s nest, الله will build for him a house in Paradise.”? “But there cannot be a mosque the size of a grouse’s nest.”

Second, it is not necessary that when one thing is stated as a conditional aspect for something else that the first thing is in itself permissible. الله’s saying, “You had given them,” does not indicate that it is permissible to give such an amount. It is like النبي ﷺ’s statement,

Oi oy Sh ee Tiss “If someone has a relative killed, he has two choices: either he can be paid the blood money or he may exact retribution.”* Furthermore, the most that one can say about this verse is that it allows the one who has the ability to pay a large dower to pay sucha dower. However, it does not mean that the one who does not have such

1 Tafseer Ayaat al-Abkaam by al-Saabooni, vol. 1, p. 453, first edition, 1391 .H./1972, Damascus. Recorded by ibn Maajah (#738) and ibn Khuzaimah (#1292) on the authority of bir ibn Abdullah. Al-Boosairi said, "Its chain is sabib." 74h -Jaami li-Abkaam al-القرآن by al Qurtubi, vol. 5, -101. * Recorded by al-Bukhari (#6880), المسلمين Cate ‘Abu Dawood (#4505), alNasaai (vol. 8, p. 38) and al-Baihagi in al-Sunan alKubra (vol. 8, p. 52).

The Figh of Marriage

ability may be forced to pay that amount. This is indicated by النبي ﷺ (عليه السلام) objecting to Abu Hadrad al-Aslami when he came to النبي ﷺ (عليه السلام) to seek assistance in paying the dower of his wife. الرسول ﷺ of الله (عليه السلام) asked him, “What is the amount of her dower?” He said, “Two hundred dirhams.” النبي ﷺ (عليه السلام) then said, “If you were to dig out [the silver] in the valley of Bathaan, you would not exceed that amount.” Recorded by al-Haakim who said that its chain is sahih although al-Bukhari and المسلمين did not record it. Al-Dhahabi concurred with his assessment.'

He also objected to the woman from the Ansar who got married with the dower being four awaag [of silver] as that was not appropriate for her situation. المسلمين recorded in his Sahih? from Abu Hurairah who said: A man came to الرسول ﷺ of الله (عليه السلام) and said, “I am getting married to a woman from the Ansar.” النبي ﷺ (عليه السلام) said to him, “Have you taken a look at her for in the eyes of the Ansaar there is something (unpleasant for those not used to it]?” He said, “I have taken a look at her.” He then said, “What was the amount [of the dower] for the marriage?” He replied, “Four ugiyas.” النبي ﷺ (عليه السلام) then said to him, “With four ugiyas? It is as if you dig out silver from the side of this mountain [and that is how you plan on paying this great amount]. We do not have anything we can give you. However, we may send you ona military expedition and you may end up getting something [that is, some booty].” النبي ﷺ (عليه السلام) sent an expedition to Banu Abs and he sent that man along with them.

Al-Nawawi wrote in his commentary to Sahih المسلمين, “The meaning of those words is the disapproval of increasing the dower telative to the situation of the husband.”

As for the story wherein Umar prohibits exorbitant dowers and then the woman refutes what he stated, although it is very famous and spread among the people, all of its chains are questionable and many

3 Al-Mustadrak ala al-Saheebain by al-Haakim al-Naisaboori, vol. 2, p. 178.

No. 1424, The Book of Marriage, Chapter on looking at the face and hands of the oman one desires to wed.

Sharh Sabeeh المسلمين by al-Nawawi, vol. 9, p. 210.

‘The Figh of Marriage

scholars have doubted it. It should be noted that it is not recorded in any source work that dates from close to the time of Umar. Furthermore, it is not recorded in the many relied upon source works of الحديث. The four companions of the Sunan works [Abu Dawood, al-Nasaai, al-Tirmidhi and ibn Maajah] all avoided recording it. Many of the leading scholars of الحديث, those who recorded Umar’s prohibition of exorbitant dowers, only record the portion where Umar speaks about the dowers related to النبي ﷺ (عليه السلام) [and they do not mention anything of the rest of the story concerning that woman].

In Ahkaam al-القرآن,! Abu Bakr ibn al-Arabi clearly stated that the well-established and known narration from Umar is that in which no woman objects to what he stated.

Furthermore, the الحديث, as recorded by the compilers of the Sunan who did not mention the story of the woman, has been narrated through al-Ajfaa ibn Naseeb and _ his الحديث are not founded and some say that there is some doubt about him.?

Some record a narration that contradicts the narration that states that a woman refuted Umar with the verse she quoted. Some, instead of having a woman rebut Umar, have the additional words, that were mentioned earlier, “A man goes to such an extreme in getting a dower for his wife that there develops an enmity for her in him, and he says, ‘I burdened myself [to the point of even providing] the rope of the waterskin for you.’””3

2 Tabdbeeb al-Tabdbeeb by ibn Hajr al-Asqilaani, vol. 14, p. 165, #790. [It is not clear to. this translator the point the author is trying to make here. Earlier, he stated in a footnote that the narration from Umar is confirmed and authentic. The questionable aspect is the additional portion, narrated by some, in which a woman confronted Umar concerning what he had said. The paragraph above does nothing to support the contention that the additional portion is not authentic, as the author has stated and shall continue to demonstrate. ‘الله knows best —JZ]

Recorded by Abu Dawood (#2106) in the Book of Marriage, Chapter on the dower; al-Tirmidhi (#1114) in the Book of Marriage, Chapter on what has been related concerning the dower of women; al-Nasaai (vol. 6, p. 117) in the Book of Marriage, Chapter on being just in the dowers, and the wording above is from him; ibn Maajah ie 887); Ahmad in al-Musnad (#285, 278, 340); and al-Haakim in al-Mustadrak vol. 2, p. 175). It is a narration that is confirmed and authentic.

‘The Fig of Marriage
